warehouse_header.notes: n=137. Bimodal: COSTCO/WHOLESALE logo lines cap_rel med 2.30, stroke_rel 1.32, reverse-video 49% (WHOLESALE band; render via logo image). Address/store lines cap_rel med 0.955, stroke_rel 0.945 (n=67) -> header text prints at ~body size.

self_checkout.notes: n=66. Bimodal: SELF-CHECKOUT heading cap_rel med 1.51 (IQR 1.47-1.53, n=23) stroke_rel 1.32 = large+heavy. 'Thank You!' lines in this section cap_rel med 0.84, stroke_rel 1.03 = SMALLER than body, normal weight.

member.notes: n=5 only; stroke_rel med 1.19 hints slightly heavy Member line, but n too small to trust.

item.notes: n=159. Body baseline by construction: cap_rel med 1.01, stroke_rel med 1.01. No underline/reverse (1 OCR-noise hit each).

items_sold.notes: n=70. Mixed: 'ITEMS SOLD:' display heading cap_rel med 1.48 (IQR 1.36-1.50, n=31) stroke_rel 1.26; plain 'TOTAL NUMBER OF ITEMS SOLD = N' lines print near body size (section p25 cap_rel 1.00).

total_line.notes: CORRECTED BY ADVERSARIAL VERIFICATION: the fleet measured 0/43 reverse because OCR cannot read white-on-black digits -- reversed regions produce NO words, so line-based scanning is structurally blind to them. Raw pixels on 2/2 held-out receipts show the TOTAL AMOUNT as white knockout digits in a solid black band; the '**** TOTAL' label prints normal-video. Field-level reversal, not whole-line.

summary.notes: n=64. SUBTOTAL/TAX at ~body size (cap_rel med 1.05, stroke_rel 1.05). No reverse, no underline.

savings.notes: n=6. INSTANT SAVINGS ~body size; 1/6 underline hit (single receipt, likely noise). n too small for a rule.

payment.notes: n=266. Body size (cap_rel med 1.00, stroke_rel 0.99). Reverse 2/266 = OCR noise.

footer.notes: n=70. OP#/Whse/Trm lines slightly smaller than body (cap_rel med 0.98, p25 0.91), stroke_rel 1.00. No styling.

barcode_caption.notes: n=34. Digits under barcode print smaller AND lighter than body: cap_rel med 0.83 (IQR 0.77-0.84), stroke_rel med 0.88. Rule set misses this entirely.

other.notes: n=395 unclassified (addresses, dates, marketing/coupon blocks). ~body size. Underline/reverse hits are coupon-graphic OCR noise.

date_box.match: date after TOTAL NUMBER OF ITEMS SOLD
date_box.notes: CORRECTED BY ADVERSARIAL VERIFICATION: date after ITEMS SOLD is boxed on 2/2 raw scans (one verifier read full reverse video, the other a printed outline box with dark digits -- treatment may vary by register). Fleet missed it: OCR garbles/drops boxed digits.

audit.display_headings.SELF-CHECKOUT=1.7: PARTIAL: large+heavy CONFIRMED (stroke_rel 1.32) but scale overstated - measured cap_rel med 1.51 (IQR 1.47-1.53, n=23); use ~1.5
audit.display_headings.THANK YOU=1.4: WRONG: 'Thank You!' measures cap_rel med 0.84 (IQR 0.79-0.87, n=30), stroke_rel 1.03 - SMALLER than body at normal weight, not a 1.4x heading
audit.display_headings.PLEASE COME AGAIN=1.4: WRONG: cap_rel med 0.84 (IQR 0.80-0.88, n=12), stroke_rel 1.11 - smaller than body, not 1.4x
audit.display_headings.ITEMS SOLD:=1.8: PARTIAL: large+heavy CONFIRMED (stroke_rel 1.26) but scale overstated - measured cap_rel med 1.48 (IQR 1.36-1.50, n=31); use ~1.5
audit.reverse_total=true: CONFIRMED by raw pixels (verifiers, 4/4 receipts) -- fleet 'WRONG' verdict was a detection failure (OCR-blind to knockout text). Nuance: reversal covers the amount field only.
audit.reverse_date_after_items=true: CONFIRMED as a boxed/reversed field by raw pixels (verifiers) -- same OCR blindness. Treatment varies: full reverse or outline box.
audit.section_scale={} (header=body): CONFIRMED: warehouse_header address lines cap_rel med 0.955, stroke_rel 0.945 (n=67) - uniform with body
audit.dashed_separators=true: UNMEASURED: scanner does not measure dash runs (separator section n=3)
audit.condense=0.93: UNMEASURED: no char-pitch data in scans
audit.underline (no rule): CONFIRMED absent: 15/~1380 lines (1.1%) underline flags, all scattered coupon/OCR noise; no systematic underlines (unlike Sprouts)
audit.MISSED: barcode_caption: Barcode digits print 0.83x body, stroke_rel 0.88, n=34 - smaller+lighter, no rule exists
audit.MISSED: thank-you sub-body size: Thank You / Please Come Again actually print ~0.84x body; rules render them 1.4x
audit.SELF-CHECKOUT=1.7: PARTIAL: real and heavy, but measured 1.5-1.65x (coded 1.7 slightly high)
audit.THANK YOU=1.4: WRONG (pixel-confirmed): prints 0.84x body, normal weight -- rule should be removed or ~0.85

limitations: stylescan iterates OCR words/lines; reverse-video and boxed fields produce no/garbled OCR and are invisible to it. Reverse-video measurement must come from raw-pixel scanning (band darkness independent of words) -- future stylescan fix.
